title: Source-grounded LLM answers with deterministic citation verification
scope: ananta hub-worker orchestration, RAG provenance, tool-run evidence, answer verification

already_existing.finding: TaskScopedExecutionService already extracts retrieval_trace_id, retrieval_context_hash and retrieval_manifest_hash from context payload metadata. This can become the anchor for answer provenance.
propose_task_step persists proposal result metadata, routing, trace and research_context. This is the right place to attach source_catalog_id / answer_contract requirements.
ContextDeliveryService supports full, none and selective context delivery and already has cloud-scope blocking behavior.
HybridRetrievalService already returns provenance entries and a deterministic retrieval_trace. This should be reused instead of inventing a second provenance system.
Existing evidence flow writes RAG artifacts, hub context, worker metadata, result hashes and evidence summary. It is a good pattern for a new Bitcoin mining citation/evidence demo.
There are already focused tests around delivery and retrieval. New tests should extend this style.

missing_or_incomplete: No explicit answer-level citation schema found.
No stable source catalog with SRC_* IDs exposed to LLM prompts found.
No explicit tool-run evidence source type RUN_* found for answers.
No answer release gate that blocks or marks unsupported claims found.
No Bitcoin mining tool-run example proving deterministic hash calculation.
context_analysis.design_principle: The LLM may formulate claims, but Ananta must own source assignment, source identity, tool-run identity and verification.

flow: hub builds SourceCatalog with stable SRC_* IDs
worker receives context plus explicit citation contract
LLM response must contain claims[] with citation_refs[]
tool executions produce RUN_* evidence records
hub validates all citation_refs against SourceCatalog and ToolRunCatalog
unsupported claims are blocked, downgraded or marked as unverified
final answer includes verified citations and verification_status
non_goals: Do not trust free-form source names generated by the LLM.
Do not let the LLM invent file paths, line ranges or tool result IDs.
Do not use heuristic source creation as default behavior.
Do not treat a natural language explanation as proof of tool execution.

tasks.title: Define source catalog schema
tasks.description: Create a deterministic schema for all sources allowed inside one task/run.
acceptance_criteria: Schema validates a catalog containing at least rag_chunk, repo_file and tool_run entries.
source_id must match deterministic IDs like SRC_0001 or RUN_0001.
content_hash is required for every source entry.
Source entries cannot omit source_type.
Schema rejects duplicate source_id values.
Schema rejects source IDs that do not match the allowed pattern.
Unit test covers valid catalog and invalid duplicate source_id.

tasks.title: Build SourceCatalogService
tasks.description: Add a service that converts retrieval selected/provenance entries into stable source catalog entries.
implementation_notes: Use provenance from HybridRetrievalService where available.
Generate stable SRC_* IDs by sorted deterministic order, not by LLM output order.
Persist catalog into task verification_status or task metadata initially; DB table can follow later.
acceptance_criteria: Given the same retrieval payload twice, SourceCatalogService returns identical source IDs and catalog hash.
Given changed selected record IDs, catalog_hash changes.
The service preserves path, record_id, manifest_hash and channel/engine metadata.
The service does not create sources for records outside selected/provenance input.
Unit tests assert deterministic ordering independent of input ordering.

tasks.title: Extend retrieval result to expose citation-ready source entries
tasks.description: Ensure retrieval output has all fields needed for source catalogs.
acceptance_criteria: Every selected retrieval item has path, record_id or content_hash.
Every provenance entry has engine, record_id, file, score and manifest_hash fields.
If line metadata exists in source metadata, it is propagated into provenance.
If line metadata does not exist, the field is null, not invented.
Existing retrieval tests still pass.

tasks.title: Define answer contract schema with claims and citations
tasks.description: Create strict JSON answer format for LLM/worker responses that can be validated.
acceptance_criteria: Schema requires every non-uncertain claim to contain at least one citation_ref.
Schema allows uncertain claims only if citation_refs is empty and confidence is unverified.
Schema rejects citation_refs that are not strings.
Schema rejects arbitrary extra top-level fields unless explicitly allowed.
Unit tests cover valid source_fact, valid tool_result and invalid uncited source_fact.

tasks.title: Add citation verification service
tasks.description: Validate grounded_answer.v1 against the SourceCatalog and ToolRunCatalog.
verification_rules: citation_ref must exist in source catalog or tool-run catalog.
source_fact claims may cite rag_chunk, repo_file, artifact or wiki_chunk.
tool_result claims must cite tool_run, test_result or generated_artifact.
A claim cannot cite a source excluded by LLM scope policy.
A citation cannot reference a source from a different task_id/run_id.
Unverified claims are allowed only when explicitly marked as unverified.
acceptance_criteria: Valid cited answer returns status verified.
Answer with unknown SRC_* returns status failed_unknown_source.
Answer citing excluded source returns status failed_policy_scope.
Answer with tool_result claim but no RUN_* citation returns status failed_missing_tool_run.
Unverified claims are preserved but marked and counted.
Verification result includes verified_claim_count, unverified_claim_count and failed_claims[].

tasks.title: Add tool run catalog for deterministic execution evidence
tasks.description: Record deterministic tool executions as source entries usable by answer citations.
acceptance_criteria: A successful Python tool execution can be recorded as RUN_0001.
stdout is not blindly trusted; stdout_hash is stored.
Large stdout can be truncated for display but full hash remains stable.
Tool run belongs to exactly one task_id.
Citation verifier accepts RUN_* for tool_result claims.

tasks.title: Inject citation contract into propose/execute prompts
tasks.description: Make the LLM aware that it must answer with source IDs supplied by Ananta, not invented paths.
prompt_contract: Every factual claim must include citation_refs.
Uncertain statements must be marked as unverified.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt includes source_catalog excerpt with SRC_* IDs.
Prompt includes answer schema name grounded_answer.v1.
Prompt explicitly forbids invented file names, line ranges and source IDs.
Existing propose path still supports command/tool_calls.
Test verifies prompt contains citation contract when source catalog is present.

tasks.title: Persist verification result in task status
tasks.description: Store answer verification metadata alongside existing verification_status/task_flow_metrics.
acceptance_criteria: After answer verification, task status contains citation_verification_status.
Failed citation verification does not silently mark task as completed.
Existing task_flow_metrics are preserved.
Read-model exposes citation verification summary.

tasks.title: Add API endpoint to inspect sources and verification
tasks.description: Expose source catalog and verification result for a task.
acceptance_criteria: GET sources returns source_catalog_id, sources[] and catalog_hash.
GET answer-verification returns status, counts and failed_claims.
Endpoint does not expose source content for sources blocked by policy.
Endpoint returns 404 for unknown task.
Route tests cover successful and missing task cases.

tasks.title: Frontend display for cited answers
tasks.description: Show citation verification status in the task UI.
acceptance_criteria: Task detail view shows verified/unverified/failed citation status.
Source refs like SRC_0001 and RUN_0001 are visible and expandable.
Unknown or failed citations are highlighted.
Blocked source content is not displayed, only metadata and reason.
Angular tests or component-level tests cover verified and failed states.

tasks.title: Bitcoin mining demo fixture
tasks.description: Create a deterministic toy Bitcoin mining example used to test tool-run evidence and citations.
demo_requirements: Use simplified artificial target, not real Bitcoin difficulty.
Build a simplified header from version, previous_block_hash, merkle_root, timestamp, bits, nonce.
Emit JSON result with nonce, hash, target, valid boolean.
Make output deterministic by fixed inputs and fixed timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Running mining_demo.py always produces the same expected_result.json.
The result includes at least one failed nonce and one valid nonce with artificial easy target.
The script states clearly that it is a toy/demo and not real Bitcoin mining.
The expected hash is verified by test, not by LLM text.
Fixture contains README text that can be indexed as SRC_* source.

tasks.title: Bitcoin mining citation/evidence unit test
tasks.description: Verify that an answer about the Bitcoin mining demo cites both explanatory source text and actual tool-run output.

test_answer_example.answer: Der Demo-Miner berechnet double_sha256 über einen vereinfachten Header und findet mit künstlich leichtem Target eine gültige Nonce.

claims.text: Der Demo-Miner verwendet double_sha256 über den Header.
Der konkrete Hash und die gültige Nonce stammen aus einem ausgeführten Tool-Run.

acceptance_criteria: Test fails if CLM_0001 cites RUN_0001 only.
Test fails if CLM_0002 cites SRC_0001 only.
Test fails if RUN_0001 does not exist in ToolRunCatalog.
Test fails if answer claims a nonce/hash not present in tool stdout/result artifact.
Test passes when SRC_0001 explains the algorithm and RUN_0001 contains matching nonce/hash evidence.

tasks.title: Bitcoin mining end-to-end evidence flow
tasks.description: Create an E2E flow similar to run_core_evidence_flow.py but focused on source-grounded answer verification.
flow_steps: Index bitcoin_mining_demo README and script as source records.
Write evidence-summary.json with catalog hash, tool-run hash and verification status.
acceptance_criteria: Script writes ci-artifacts/bitcoin-mining-citation-evidence/evidence-summary.json.
Evidence summary contains source_catalog_hash.
Evidence summary contains RUN_0001 stdout_hash.
Evidence summary contains citation_verification_status=verified.
E2E test fails if answer contains uncited factual claim.
E2E test fails if answer cites invented SRC_9999.

tasks.title: Negative tests for hallucinated citations
tasks.description: Ensure source-grounding cannot be bypassed by plausible-looking LLM output.
negative_cases: citation to source from different task
tool result claim without tool run
claim text says hash was computed but only README is cited
acceptance_criteria: Every negative case returns failed or unverified, never verified.
Failure reason is deterministic and machine-readable.
No negative test relies on LLM judgement.
Tests are stable without network access.

tasks.title: Policy integration for local/cloud source visibility
tasks.description: Ensure citations cannot reveal sources that were not allowed for the worker/LLM scope.
acceptance_criteria: If full context is blocked for external cloud, source catalog marks those sources as not allowed.
Citation verifier rejects citations to not-allowed sources.
API source inspection hides content for blocked sources.
Existing local_only behavior remains unchanged.
Tests cover external_cloud_allowed with allow_full_context_for_cloud=false.

tasks.title: Documentation for deterministic source-grounding
tasks.description: Document that source-grounding is enforced by Ananta, not trusted from the LLM.
must_document: LLM may only cite provided SRC_*/RUN_* IDs.
No heuristic source invention by default.
Bitcoin mining demo as minimal deterministic example.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include one valid grounded_answer.v1 example.
Docs include one invalid hallucinated citation example.
Docs explain difference between SRC_* and RUN_*.
Docs mention retrieval_trace_id/context_hash/manifest_hash.
AGENTS.md states that source IDs must not be invented.

bitcoin_test_example.purpose: Prove that Ananta can distinguish between LLM text explanation and deterministic tool evidence.
expected_answer_behavior: Claim about the algorithm cites README/script source.
Claim about concrete nonce/hash cites tool-run output.
Claim that this is real Bitcoin mining is rejected or marked unverified.
Claim that no tool was needed is rejected if a tool_result claim is present.

definition_of_done: grounded_answer.v1 schema exists and is tested.
CitationVerificationService exists and rejects hallucinated citations.
Propose/execute prompt includes citation contract when source catalog exists.
Bitcoin mining demo produces deterministic tool-run evidence.
E2E evidence flow proves verified answer with SRC_* and RUN_* refs.
